clinical_history: 60 year old woman with palpitations and rapid pulse at rest. The ECG shows which one of the following arrhythmias?
diagnosis: The ECG shows a very regular narrow complex tachycardia, ventricular rate of 200 bpm. The rate is too fast for resting sinus tachycardia in a patient of this age group. The rhythm is too regular for atrial fibrillation or MAT.  The rate is not compatible with atrial flutter with 2:1 conduction since the atrial rate would have to be 400 cycles/min, beyond the atrial depolarization of that rhythm. By exclusion the rhythm is paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia. No definite P waves are evident and the mechanism is likely AV nodal reentry (with P waves hidden in the QRS or perhaps partly visible at the very end of QRS with a superior axis). The ST depressions are non-diagnostic in this context.  For a superb tutorial and simulation of AVNRT and other supraventricular tachycardias, see: http://www.blaufuss.org/SVT/index2.html
